MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT JEAN BOLDAN'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

is OFF CALENDAR. The motions for judgment on the pleadings were previously taken off calendar pending the final resolution of the Federal Action. Notice of Motion does not address this issue. The following language (quoted verbatim) in the Request for Judicial Notice at page 2 lines 15-19 did not provide any information regarding the condition precedent to re-filing of the motion for judgment on the pleadings, i.e. a final resolution of the Federal Action: "The matter was argued on November 6, 2020, and the Court issued its ruling on the instant proceedings the out court of the U.S. District Court matter. A true copy of the Court's instant action as exhibit H to the moving parties Omnibus Request for Judicial Notice indicates the moving parties can re-file/notice Motions for judgment on the pleadings." To the extent the Federal Action has not been finally resolved, November 6, 2022 ruling stands. If the action has been finally resolved, D may file a new motion clearly stating so in the Notice of the Motion or in the opening paragraph on the Points and Authorities and addressing what impact, if any, the final resolution has on this case.
The Court notes that no courtesy copies of opposition was received.
